text:
Athens Times

—

He seems to be preparing to put a “deep” hand in the pocket, after its president, Pinto Da Costa effectively announced David Carmo’s transfer to the Pirates. Karmos was acquired by Olympiacos only last January, but the “red” FC appears ready to pay the dizzying sum of 17.5 million euros of its clause, to acquire it from Porto with a regular transfer. At least that is what the president of Porto said, who said: “We spent 20 million on Carmo, but we are not going to lose it. Olympiacos has informed us that he will pay the 17.5 million of his appearance.” Olympiacos will thus make the 24-year-old defender the most expensive transfer of Super League 1, “singing” and its great reseller value, due to his young age.

Athens Times

News about Greece and the World.
title: President Porto: "Olympic informed he would activate David Carmo's clause" - Athens Times
author: uploader
